summary: Fourth pass at cleaning up item headers and item struct usage. The PR renamed `src/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h` to `src/dolphin/mtx/types.h`, updated many includes across fighter, stage, item, GX, lb, mp, and baselib code, introduced item forward declarations and item enum definitions in `src/melee/it/forward.h`, added out-of-line Doxygen documentation files for item forward/types material, and replaced many raw `Item` field names in `item.c`/`item2.c` with clearer names such as `kind`, `entity`, `asid`, `anim_id`, `scl`, `spin_spd`, `bones`, `count`, `dyn_descs`, and `child_count`. It also adjusted Doxygen enum formatting and updated symbol-replacement tooling to cover `.dox` and `.md` files while dropping the old `include` directory from the search path.
what_changed: Renamed the matrix type header from `src/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h` to `src/dolphin/mtx/types.h` and updated include sites throughout the repo to use `<dolphin/mtx/types.h>`.
Changed the renamed mtx header guard from `_MTXTYPES_h_` to `DOLPHIN_MTX_TYPES_H`, matching the project-style path-based guard convention shown in nearby headers.
Added item forward declarations and enums to `src/melee/it/forward.h`, including `Tangibility`, `Item_StateChangeFlags`, and `Item_UnkKinds`.
Added `src/melee/it/forward.dox` and `src/melee/it/types.dox` for out-of-line Doxygen documentation of item enums, flags, and types.
Updated `Doxyfile` so `ENUM_VALUES_PER_LINE` is `1`, making generated enum docs display one value per line.

Refined item struct field names in item code: examples include `x10_item_kind` to `kind`, `x4_GObj` to `entity`, `x24_item_state_index` to `asid`, `x28_item_anim_index` to `anim_id`, `x38_scale` to `scl`, and `x34_spin_speed` to `spin_spd`.

Refined dynamic-bone/item-dynamics naming: examples include dynamic bone table `x0_jobj` to `bones`, dynamics count fields to `count`, dynamics descriptor arrays to `dyn_descs`, and descriptor child count to `child_count`.
Adjusted item initialization and state-change code in `item.c` to use the newly named fields while preserving existing behavior.
Updated `tools/functions.sh` replacement script to search `asm` and `src`, include `.dox` and `.md` files, and no longer search the removed/unused `include` directory.
smart_moves: Kept the mtx type header rename centralized and mechanical by updating all include users, reducing the risk of split include names for the same type definitions.
Moved item enum documentation toward out-of-line `.dox` files, which keeps headers cleaner while preserving documentation for generated Doxygen output.
Used semantic field names in hot item code rather than only documenting offsets, making future decomp work easier to read without changing function behavior.
Updated the repository symbol-replacement helper to include documentation files, which is important when names are propagated into `.dox`/`.md` as part of decomp cleanup.
Adjusted Doxygen formatting for enums to one value per line, which better supports large flag enums like `Item_StateChangeFlags`.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a foundational header, update every transitive include site in the same PR and align the include guard to the new path/name.
Forward headers are a good home for lightweight typedefs and enums that many item files need, especially when trying to reduce dependency pressure from a large `item.h`/`types.h`.
Field renames should be propagated into all active users immediately; examples in this PR show `Item.kind`, `Item.scl`, `Item.asid`, and `Item.anim_id` replacing offset-style names in item logic.

Out-of-line Doxygen is useful when headers become crowded with enum and struct documentation; however, the review comment notes this was decided midway through the PR, so some inline docs were expected to be moved later.
Tooling used for symbol replacement must search docs as well as C/header/asm files once names are documented outside code.
naming_conventions: Use path-based all-caps include guards such as `DOLPHIN_MTX_TYPES_H` for renamed headers.
Prefer generic library header names like `<dolphin/mtx/types.h>` over narrower legacy names like `<dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h>` when the header is the type umbrella for a subsystem.

Use concise semantic item fields where evidence supports them: `kind` for item kind, `entity` for owning `HSD_GObj`, `asid` for item state index, `anim_id` for animation id/index, `scl` for scale, and `spin_spd` for spin speed.
Use plural/container names for arrays and counts in descriptors: `bones`, `count`, `dyn_descs`, and `child_count`.
Item state-change flags use `ITEM_*` all-caps enum constants with either action names (`ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE`, `ITEM_CMD_UPDATE`) or preservation names (`ITEM_HIT_PRESERVE`, `ITEM_SFX_PRESERVE`).
Unknown item enum names remained explicitly marked as unknown where semantics were incomplete, e.g. `ITEM_UNK_0x1`, `ITEM_UNK_UPDATE`, `ITEM_UNK_MATO`, `ITEM_UNK_LOCKON`, and `ITEM_UNK_ENEMY`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff excerpt shows mostly header/include cleanup and field renaming, not explicit assembly matching tactics.
Behavior-preserving renames in `item.c` demonstrate a low-risk matching tactic: improve struct names while leaving control flow, constants, and function calls intact.
The removal of a local `extern HSD_JObj* HSD_JObjAlloc();` in the excerpt suggests relying on proper declarations from headers when available, but the evidence is limited to one removed extern line.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: We decided on out-of-line docs midway through the creation of this PR so I'll move these over later.
review_feedback.lesson: Documentation placement was still in transition; future cleanup should continue moving inline docs to `.dox` when that is the project convention.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.lesson: Symbol replacement tooling should track the repository's current source/documentation layout.

searchable_terms: dynamic bones bones count dyn_descs child_count
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete moving inline item docs from `src/melee/it/types.h` into `.dox` files.
Search for remaining includes of `<dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h>` after PR 679.
Search for remaining offset-style item fields such as `x10_item_kind`, `x38_scale`, `x24_item_state_index`, and `x28_item_anim_index` after this PR.
Check whether `Item_StateChangeFlags` bit names were further refined from unknown names like `ITEM_UNK_0x1` and `ITEM_UNK_UPDATE`.
Review later item header passes for additional `Item` struct field naming conventions.
